routing_decision: Route directly to Technical Discovery (impact-mode) without dispatching a Researcher. The escalation is a coverage/spec-shape gap spanning the section list and contracts (category=prose_coverage_gap), which is the Sev 2b coverage-spanning variant. Per the Arbiter charter note for this case, the resolution is mechanical: add additive `covers` back-pointers from new or existing discovery_coverage_assertions / per-section-coverage assertions to (a) each section charter, (b) each per-section out_of_scope item, and (c) each contract interface method/field plus `.notes`. No new research, no Discovery amendment, and no plan-shaking is required because no behavior, contract, or scope is changing (escalation confirms no_behavior_regression, no_contract_violation, no_out_of_scope_leak).
rationale: Category `prose_coverage_gap` spans multiple sections (1-4) and all contracts, making it cross-section/coverage-spanning rather than a two-section data-shape change (rules out 2a) or a single-section local fix (rules out 1). It does not reference a Discovery assumption nor require re-deriving the plan (rules out 3); it is not user-facing irreconcilable (rules out 4); it is not trivial-already-applied (rules out 0). High Critic severity is the v1.7 mandated flag for any coverage-required-field gap regardless of implicit satisfaction, so it must be processed even though behavior is correct. Researcher dispatch skipped because the fix space is fully constrained by file_schemas.md § Coverage-Required Fields and the existing artifacts; no investigation is needed.
expected_resolution_path: TD (impact-mode) authors additive coverage assertions whose `covers` fields name: (1) sections[].charter for sections 1-4; (2) every sections[].out_of_scope[] item across sections 1-4; (3) every contract interface method/field (fetchEarthquakes and EarthquakeEvent shape fields on contract.section-1--section-2 and contract.section-1--section-3; mount, magnitudeColor, magnitudeRadius on contract.section-2--section-3; integratedArtifact on contract.integrator--section-4) plus each contract's `.notes` block. Assertions may re-use existing structural/absence checks; no rebuild is required. TD then returns to Coordinator for Critic re-sweep against the augmented coverage set.
